title: leucas ebracteata var. ebracteata
intro: Leucas ebracteata var. ebracteata is a perennial plant species, commonly known as the "Nerium" or "Asteraceae" family plant, native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Indian subcontinent and parts of Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenoids and other compounds. The health benefits of Leucas ebracteata var. ebracteata include its use in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Leucas ebracteata var. ebracteata can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to leverage its medicinal properties.
